title: After-Action Report — Per-League Playoffs, Configurable Format & Separate Postseason Stats
scope: Rewrote playoff engine for per-league brackets, configurable series lengths, and separate postseason stats tracking with live UI controls

rationale.item: Playoff model was fundamentally broken: unclear what postseason was, no per-league structure
AL and NL were thrown into one pool with no WS between league champions
Series lengths were hardcoded and not configurable

delegation_events.decision: Per-league brackets as primary model; cross-league WS only for 2-league saves
delegation_events.quote: Each league crowns its own champion. Leagues are nationally independent — a club in one league never meets a club in another during a bracket. Only when a single save holds exactly two leagues (the AL/NL case) do the two champions then play a World Series

delegation_events.decision: Default series ladder 3/5/7/7 (Wild Card/Division/League Championship/World Series)
Playoff stats tracked separately, never double-counted
delegation_events.quote: Regular-season / career / leaderboard numbers exclude playoff games; postseason gets its own tab and page

actions.description: Rewrote o27v2/playoffs.py with per-league bracket engine
Added league and series_kind columns to playoff_series table
Fixed champion tracking in season_archive.py to use postseason champion
Implemented playoff settings persistence in /playoffs/settings route
Added is_playoff flag to game_batter_stats and game_pitcher_stats
Updated ~51 query sites to filter out playoff games for regular-season stats
Created Postseason tab on player card for playoff-only stats

risk_posture: Deliberately left in-season roster modeling unfiltered; operates on raw stat tables during regular season only
Stat invariants 5 and 8 re-scoped to regular-season-only games

validation_claims.claim: End-to-end on 8-team save: AL and NL each ran 4-team series, World Series winner crowned
validation_claims.check_method: Run 8teams config full season then drain bracket

validation_claims.claim: Configurable format: /playoffs/settings POST persisted custom ladder
validation_claims.check_method: Set teams_per_league=6 and 5/7/9/9 ladder, verify reflection
Verify standard seed order and bye placement

validation_claims.claim: Stat invariants: 9/9 pass on played-out save
validation_claims.check_method: Run invariant harness on test DB

non_actions.item: In-season roster modeling scoped to regular season
non_actions.reason: Runs during regular season when no playoff rows exist yet

residual_risks: 3+ co-equal leagues with bracket enabled crown independent champions with no overall champion
Postseason leaderboard thresholds are minimal (≥1 PA/≥1 out) due to tiny postseason samples
Two pre-existing test failures remain (test_managers.py, test_trades.py) unrelated to this work
open_hooks: Scope in-season roster modeling (development.py, trades.py, injuries.py, rotation.py, bench.py) to regular season
section_headers_original: After-Action Report — Per-League Playoffs, Configurable Format & Separate Postseason Stats
What the old model did (and why it felt broken)
6. Stat invariants scoped to the regular season
parser_notes: Comprehensive overhaul of playoff system with clear design decisions confirmed with user. Stat filtering touch was broad (~51 sites) and mechanical, addressed via shared helpers. Pre-existing test failures noted as out of scope.
